source: Everyday words the course needs that the VOA1500 news wordlist lacks
why: VOA1500 is a NEWS vocabulary: it carries administration/guerrilla/asylum but not breakfast/park/sorry/phone. The blueprint's selection rule is the opposite — how often does a child actually meet this word — so 36% of the words its lesson cards name are absent from the list, and its passages could not even use them. Human ruling (2026-07-22): 以蓝图为准, whatever the blueprint requires must be supplemented, no quantity limit.
policy: These are first-class course vocabulary: passages may use them and lessons may teach them. Coverage of the original 1541 VOA words is still reported separately, so the 100%-VOA goal is unaffected.
derivedFormsCovered: Base forms are listed (tooth/mouse/goose/shelf/leaf/sock/stair/wake/draw/lay/ring/thief) so their irregular inflections resolve through the lemma rules rather than needing their own entries.
